text: For every performer you see on stage, there are dozens of invisible workers making the show possible.
Lighting designers, riggers, stagehands, and dressers often outnumber the cast on a big production.
Most audience members never learn the names of the people who build, light, and move the world of a show.
A single costume can pass through the hands of pattern cutters, dyers, stitchers, and dressers before opening night.
Backstage crews rehearse scene changes like choreography so each blackout feels like magic.
